Most Trials Fail Because We Treat the Wrong Patients

  • Response rates stall ~50% across diseases
  • Responders diluted by non-responders
  • Signal exists — hidden in the cohort
Same drug.
Wrong patients.

Patient Biology Has Structure

  • Patients organize into a shared biological space
  • No labels required
  • Consistent across datasets

Response is

not random.

Response Is Movement — Not Classification

  • Each patient = a coordinate
  • Measured relative to a stable baseline (Z_ref)
  • Response = trajectory

Outcomes follow

geometry.

Not All Treatments Work the Same Way

  • Some treatments require precise alignment
  • Others require global biological shift

Directional

Rapid, Mechanism-Specific

Diffuse

Slow, System-Wide

Selecting the Right Patients Improves Outcomes

  • SSRI: ~1.1–1.3x
  • Ketamine: 1.48x
  • Crohn’s (TNF): 1.36x
  • Oncology (PD-1): ~1.3x

Validated across independent datasets (strict out-of-fold)

Non-Responders Are Not Random

  • Large subset of patients show limited benefit
  • Non-response follows organized structures

Hidden Responders Can Be Identified

  • Patients align to different mechanisms
  • Can be re-selected for better fit

Failed trials contain

success.

Patients Align to Different Treatments

  • Patients misaligned with one therapy may respond to another

Patients don't fail treatment —

Treatments fail alignment.

One System — Across Diseases

  • Locked transformation
  • Works across independent datasets

Same system.

Everywhere.

Deploy From Blood

  • Input: blood sample
  • Output: patient–treatment alignment
  • No retraining required
  • No indication-specific models

The Right Patients Change Everything

  • Trials fail due to selection
  • Biology provides structure
  • We make it actionable

Validated across diseases. No retraining required.

Select the right patients →
change the outcome.

Everyone Looks for Biomarkers. We Model the System.

Biomarker / Diagnostics
  • Small gene sets
  • Disease-specific
  • Fragile across datasets
Outcome: inconsistent, non-generalizable
ML Prediction Models
  • Trained per dataset
  • Requires retraining
  • Captures correlation, not structure
Outcome: unstable, not transferable
BlueMoon System
  • Full-system mapping (~18k genes → 16D)
  • Fixed projection (no retraining)
  • Captures biological structure
Outcome: consistent enrichment across diseases
Others chase signals. We map the system that generates them.

What This Enables

  • Expansion across additional diseases and cohorts
  • Integration into clinical trial design workflows
  • Longitudinal tracking of patient trajectories
  • Reduced-input deployment (future work)

Built as a system — not a single use case
